rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK VS. XELAN PROP 1, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 25, 2020 line 4. Motion For Summary Judgment Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail addresses provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment is GRANTED. Motion for Summary Judgment is framed by the pleadings. There are no issues remaining to be tried in this action on the two causes of action alleged in the complaint. Late-filed, oversized opposition without a table of contents was considered. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA LUBARSKY VS. JOHN STEWART COMPANY A CALAIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 25, 2020 line 1. Defendant'S General Demurrer Complaint is continued to October 1, 2020. On July 31, 2020 the Court requested that courtesy copies of all filings in support of and in opposition to the motion be delivered to the department by August 7, 2020 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. The Court did not receive courtesy copies. Parties are ordered to deliver courtesy copies of their respective filings no later than 9:30 a.m. on August 25, 2020 with cover letters reflecting new hearing date. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCINE VERDIER VS. TONY SINGH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Relief Under Ccp 473
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 25, 2020 line 3. PLAINTIFF FRANCINE VERDIER Notice Of Motion For Relief Under Ccp 473 Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail addresses provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Plaintiff's Motion for Relief under CCP 473(b). is GRANTED as follows: (1) July 8, 2020 Order is set aside; (2) Demurrer and Joinder to demurrer are set for hearing on September 18, 2020; (3) by no later than August 24, 2020 Plaintiff shall file its opposition to demurrer/joinder; (4) by no later than August 31, 2020 defendants shall file replies, if any. = (501/CFH)
